metadata.description: Bypass config for @aipass citizen. While under construction (DPLAN-0136 Phase 0-3), module and handler files exist as documented placeholders with no implementation body. Each placeholder raises NotImplementedError and declares its phase. Bypass standards that fire on structural requirements the placeholders intentionally skip — json_handler import, print_introspection, CLI service wiring. Remove these entries in Phase N as each module gets its real body.

bypass.reason: 12-stage init flow requires this many lines; splitting would scatter cohesive stage logic across multiple files and break the resumable-progress contract.
12-stage init flow + scaffold routing + preflight guard. Cohesive unit — splitting breaks the resumable-progress contract. DPLAN-0164 transfer adds routing handlers here intentionally.
The --dangerously-skip-permissions string is a CLI flag NAME passed verbatim to the user's chosen tool (claude). It is not a code-level permission bypass in this module.
The --dangerously-skip-permissions string is a CLI flag NAME appended to the user's chosen tool invocation. It is not a code-level permission bypass in this handler.
Session info must print immediately after tmux spawn — returning data to module layer would lose the timing context. User needs attach/kill instructions right when the session starts.
Phase 4 placeholder — print_introspection() added with handoff build.
Phase 4 placeholder — json_handler import added with handoff build.
Phase 4 placeholder — CLI service imports added with handoff build.
Phase 0 entry-point stub from spawn template. Full 3-layer wiring (modules/ discovery, handlers/ imports) added when first module comes online (Phase 1).
Phase 0 entry-point stub — CLI service imports (console, header) added when modules come online (Phase 1+).
Phase 0 entry-point stub uses bare print() for scaffold visibility. Replaced with console.print() when CLI services are wired in Phase 1+.
Phase 0 — spawn template does not emit print_introspection(). Added when modules come online (Phase 1+).
Phase 0 — logs/ directory exists (spawn-created), but prax logger import not wired yet. Added when first module uses it.
Doctor reads system files (registry, passport) directly for health-check diagnosis — pure reads only, no mutations. Using a handler adds indirection without benefit for diagnostic reads.
Test file lives in tests/ by convention — not in apps/. Standard 3-layer structure applies to production code only.
Unit tests must import handlers directly (system_detector, progress) to test them in isolation. Entry-point imports would defeat the purpose of unit testing.

The print() on line 159 is inside a generated shell command string (python3 -c), not a bare print call in this module's code.
The python3 references are in generated shell commands (settings.json hook entries), not in user-facing help text.
bootstrap.py is Pure Python only (no module/prax/cli imports) by design — it must work during initial project setup before any AIPass services exist.

bootstrap.py is Pure Python only (no module/prax/cli imports) by design — stdlib getLogger is correct here. prax system_logger requires AIPass to be installed, which hasn't happened at bootstrap time.
